howItWorks.intro: This golf handicap calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Differential
rules.explanation: Adjusted score differential normalized by slope.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a golf handicap?
faq.answer: A number representing your playing ability. Lower handicap = better golfer.

faq.question: How many rounds do I need?
faq.answer: Minimum 3 rounds, officially 20 rounds for full calculation.

faq.question: Is the Golf Handicap Index Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What score 1 should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate score 1 value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Golf Handicap Index Calculator?
sections.body: The Golf Handicap Index Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your score 1, score 2, score 3, you get instant results including average differential, handicap index. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting average differential right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your score 1 and need to find the right average differential. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Score 1
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your score 1 — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: score 2, score 3, course rating, slope rating. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your average differential, handicap index. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your score 1 before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both average differential and handicap index — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to analyze performance.
If you're unsure about your slope rating, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
